resources.title: GST Rate Changes for FY 2025-26: What Businesses Need to Know
resources.author: Dave and Associates Team
resources.excerpt: Comprehensive overview of the latest GST rate changes announced for FY 2025-26 and their impact on various business sectors.
resources.content:
The Government of India has announced significant changes to GST rates effective from April 1, 2026. These changes aim to simplify the tax structure and provide relief to certain sectors while ensuring adequate revenue collection.

Key Changes

The GST Council has revised rates across multiple categories:

  • Healthcare Services: Several essential healthcare services have been exempted from GST to reduce the burden on common citizens.
  • Textile Industry: The GST rate on textiles has been rationalized to boost the domestic textile industry.
  • E-commerce Sector: New provisions for TCS (Tax Collected at Source) have been introduced for e-commerce operators.
  • Real Estate: Input Tax Credit (ITC) provisions have been clarified for the construction sector.

Impact on Businesses

Businesses need to update their accounting systems and ensure compliance with the new rates. It's crucial to:

  1. Review your product/service classifications
  2. Update billing software with new GST rates
  3. Train your accounts team on the changes
  4. File transition forms if applicable

How We Can Help

Dave & Associates provides comprehensive GST advisory services to help your business navigate these changes smoothly. Our team can assist with compliance, return filing, and strategic tax planning.

For detailed consultation, please contact us.

resources.title: Understanding MSME Subsidy Schemes in Gujarat 2026
resources.author: CA Sarang Dave
resources.excerpt: A complete guide to MSME subsidy schemes available in Gujarat, including eligibility criteria and application process.
resources.content:
The Gujarat Government offers various attractive subsidy schemes for Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) to promote industrial growth and employment generation in the state.

Major Subsidy Schemes

1. Capital Subsidy

MSMEs can avail capital subsidy ranging from 25% to 50% on fixed capital investment, depending on the location and category of the unit.

2. Interest Subsidy

Interest subsidy is provided to reduce the financial burden on term loans taken for setting up new units or expansion of existing units.

3. SGST Reimbursement

Manufacturing units can get reimbursement of State GST paid for a period of up to 7 years.

4. Power Tariff Subsidy

Special power tariff subsidy is available for certain categories of industries to reduce operational costs.

Eligibility Criteria

  • Unit should be registered as MSME with Udyam Registration
  • Unit should be located in designated industrial areas
  • Minimum investment criteria should be met
  • Unit should commence production within specified timelines

Application Process

  1. Obtain Udyam Registration Certificate
  2. Prepare detailed project report
  3. Apply online through the state industrial portal
  4. Submit required documents and certificates
  5. Track application status regularly

Our Expertise

Dave & Associates has successfully helped numerous MSMEs in Gujarat avail these subsidies. Our end-to-end services include eligibility assessment, documentation, application filing, and liaison with government departments.

resources.title: Income Tax Deductions Every Business Owner Should Know
resources.author: CA Stuti R. Bhatt
resources.excerpt: Maximize your tax savings with these often-overlooked income tax deductions available for business owners and professionals.
resources.content:
As a business owner, understanding available tax deductions is crucial for optimizing your tax liability. Here are some important deductions you shouldn't miss:

Business Expense Deductions

1. Office Expenses

Rent, electricity, internet, telephone, and other office maintenance expenses are fully deductible.

2. Employee Salaries and Benefits

All payments made to employees including salaries, bonuses, and statutory contributions (PF, ESI) are deductible.

3. Professional Fees

Fees paid to chartered accountants, lawyers, consultants, and other professionals are allowable deductions.

4. Travel and Conveyance

Business-related travel expenses including fuel, vehicle maintenance, and travel tickets are deductible.

Investment-Based Deductions

Section 80C

Investments in PPF, ELSS, life insurance premiums, and principal repayment of home loans qualify for deduction up to ₹1.5 lakhs.

Section 80D

Health insurance premiums paid for self, family, and parents are deductible up to ₹75,000.

Section 80CCD(1B)

Additional deduction of ₹50,000 for contributions to National Pension Scheme (NPS).

Depreciation Benefits

Businesses can claim depreciation on assets like computers, furniture, vehicles, and machinery as per prescribed rates.

Tax Planning Tips

  • Maintain proper documentation for all expenses
  • Plan investments before financial year-end
  • Utilize presumptive taxation schemes if eligible
  • Consider business structure (proprietorship vs company) for tax optimization

Professional Assistance

Tax planning requires expertise and timely action. Dave & Associates offers personalized tax advisory services to help you maximize deductions legally and efficiently.

resources.title: Virtual CFO Services: Do You Need One for Your Business?
resources.author: Dave and Associates Team
resources.excerpt: Explore how Virtual CFO services can transform your business finances without the cost of a full-time CFO.
resources.content:
In today's competitive business environment, sound financial management is crucial for success. However, many small and medium businesses cannot afford a full-time Chief Financial Officer (CFO). This is where Virtual CFO services come in.

What is a Virtual CFO?

A Virtual CFO is an outsourced financial expert who provides CFO-level services to businesses on a part-time or project basis. They bring strategic financial planning and management expertise without the overhead of a full-time executive.

Services Provided

Financial Planning & Analysis

  • Cash flow management and forecasting
  • Budget preparation and monitoring
  • Financial modeling for business decisions
  • Profitability analysis

Strategic Advisory

  • Business expansion planning
  • Cost optimization strategies
  • Investment decisions
  • Funding and capital structure planning

Compliance & Reporting

  • MIS reporting
  • Financial statements preparation
  • Regulatory compliance
  • Board presentations

Who Needs a Virtual CFO?

Virtual CFO services are ideal for:

  • Startups looking to scale up
  • Growing SMEs with complex financial needs
  • Family businesses seeking professional financial management
  • Companies preparing for fundraising or exit

Benefits

  1. Cost-Effective: Pay only for the services you need
  2. Expertise on Demand: Access to experienced professionals
  3. Scalable: Services can grow with your business
  4. Objective Perspective: Unbiased financial advice

Why Choose Dave & Associates?

Our Virtual CFO services combine technical expertise with practical business acumen. We work closely with business owners to understand their goals and provide tailored financial solutions.

resources.title: ROC Compliance Checklist for Private Limited Companies 2026
resources.author: CA Stuti R. Bhatt
resources.excerpt: Complete ROC compliance checklist for private limited companies to ensure timely filing and avoid penalties.
resources.content:
Private Limited Companies in India must comply with various filing requirements under the Companies Act, 2013. Non-compliance can result in heavy penalties and legal complications.

Annual Filings

1. Financial Statements (Form AOC-4)

Due Date: Within 30 days from the date of AGM
Documents Required: Audited Balance Sheet, P&L Account, Cash Flow Statement, Director's Report, Auditor's Report

2. Annual Return (Form MGT-7)

Due Date: Within 60 days from the date of AGM
Documents Required: Details of members, directors, shareholding pattern, changes during the year

3. Director's Report

Must include details about company operations, financial performance, changes in directors, loans given, etc.

Event-Based Filings

Change in Directors (Form DIR-12)

Within 30 days of appointment/resignation

Change in Registered Office (Form INC-22)

Within 30 days of change

Allotment of Shares (Form PAS-3)

Within 30 days of allotment

Charge Creation (Form CHG-1)

Within 30 days of charge creation

Board Meetings

Minimum 4 board meetings must be held in a year with a maximum gap of 120 days between two meetings.

Annual General Meeting (AGM)

Must be held within 6 months from the end of financial year and within 15 months from the previous AGM.

Penalties for Non-Compliance

  • Late filing fees ranging from ₹100 to ₹200 per day
  • Penalties on company and officers
  • Disqualification of directors
  • Possible striking off of company name

Best Practices

  1. Maintain a compliance calendar
  2. Hold regular board meetings
  3. Ensure timely statutory audits
  4. Keep proper books of accounts
  5. File forms well before due dates

Our Services

Dave & Associates provides comprehensive ROC compliance services including annual filings, event-based filings, and compliance audits. Our team ensures your company remains fully compliant with all statutory requirements.

resources.title: Transfer Pricing: A Guide for International Transactions
resources.author: CA Sarang Dave
resources.excerpt: Understanding transfer pricing regulations and compliance requirements for businesses engaged in international transactions.
resources.content:
Transfer pricing has become increasingly important for businesses engaged in international transactions. With stringent regulations and heavy penalties for non-compliance, it's crucial to understand the basics.

What is Transfer Pricing?

Transfer pricing refers to the pricing of transactions between related parties (like parent company and subsidiary) across borders. These transactions must be conducted at arm's length price - the price that would be charged between unrelated parties.

Applicability

Transfer pricing provisions apply to:

  • International transactions between associated enterprises
  • Specified domestic transactions exceeding prescribed thresholds

Key Compliance Requirements

1. Transfer Pricing Study

Detailed documentation supporting the arm's length nature of transactions

2. Form 3CEB

Accountant's report on international transactions and specified domestic transactions

3. Country-by-Country Report (CbCR)

Required for large multinational groups with consolidated revenue exceeding ₹6400 crores

Transfer Pricing Methods

  1. Comparable Uncontrolled Price (CUP)
  2. Resale Price Method (RPM)
  3. Cost Plus Method (CPM)
  4. Profit Split Method (PSM)
  5. Transactional Net Margin Method (TNMM)

Penalties for Non-Compliance

  • 2% of value of international transaction for failure to maintain documentation
  • Transfer pricing adjustments leading to additional tax liability
  • Penalty of 100-300% of tax on concealed income

Safe Harbour Rules

To reduce litigation, the government has introduced safe harbour rules for certain categories of transactions. If the taxpayer opts for safe harbour, their transfer pricing will not be questioned.

Our Expertise

Dave & Associates provides comprehensive transfer pricing services including:

  • Transfer pricing study and documentation
  • Benchmarking analysis
  • Form 3CEB certification
  • Assistance in transfer pricing assessments and appeals
  • Advance Pricing Agreement (APA) applications
